title: A mega review of a mega dungeon crawl - Ker Nethalas: Into the Midnight Throne
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Y7Rm_Bmu-K0

Daniel (@dungeondive) delivers a mega-review of Ker Nethalas: Into the Midnight Throne by Alex T (Blackoath Entertainment) — a complex, deeply crunchy single-player dungeon-crawl RPG of brutal survival in a sprawling mega-dungeon of linked domains, each ruled by an overseer. Your character, a botched execution, wakes in the pit with nothing but wits and starting gear.

He calls it one of the cheapest ways to a vast solo experience — the single book overflows with content — while candidly flagging organisation gripes that hobbled his first game (gear and personal goals are buried far from character creation; the masteries section is easy to misread). The systems are rich: skills-as-stats on a d100 roll-under, throttled damage tables, replenishing ether that *encourages* using abilities, hit-location combat, 20 masteries (three active at once via amulets), and personal goals as character-shaping side quests. The dungeon feels alive — overseer influences, a growing-darkness track, tension and lair dice — and the bestiary and event decks are superb. The optional card set (every chart printed, page numbers on each card) gives it a near-board-game feel; a fast-combat option suits filming.

A standout entry in the pen-and-paper solo-crawl niche he plans to compare with Four Against Darkness, 2D6 Dungeon and the rest.

----

Does Ker Nethalas's crunch and living-dungeon simulation thrill you, or push past your tolerance for fiddly solo combat?
